tasks.description: Implement auto-decomposition trigger in orchestrator after 5 rejections
tasks.context: In backend/src/services/orchestrator.ts, add rejection counter to task state. After 5 consecutive rejections, automatically call decomposition logic with flag autoDecomposed=true. Store rejection reasons in task metadata for decomposition context. Reset counter on acceptance.

review.summary: Dual-rejected: Claude (25/100) + Codex (20/100). Code is incomplete - file cuts off mid-function without closing braces, making it syntactically invalid. Cannot evaluate full implementation. | The generated orchestrator changes introduce the new TaskState structure and rejection counting, but the core auto-decomposition logic after five rejections is missing, and the counter is never reset on acceptance. There are also no tests covering this new behavior.

issues.description: File is incomplete - cuts off in the middle of handleTaskRejection method without proper closing braces or method completion
Missing implementation of auto-decomposition logic when TASK_REJECTION_THRESHOLD (5) is reached
Missing implementation of decomposition service integration and decomposition logic calling
No test files provided despite this being core business logic that requires comprehensive testing
TaskState interface missing persistence - only stored in memory Map, will be lost on service restart
No check against TASK_REJECTION_THRESHOLD (5) and no invocation of the decomposition logic with autoDecomposed=true. The spec-required automatic decomposition path is entirely absent.
handleTaskSuccess does not reset the taskState.rejectionCount or clear rejectionReasons, contrary to the requirement to reset on acceptance.
No unit tests (Jest) were provided to validate the new rejection-counter and auto-decomposition behaviors, violating the >80% coverage requirement.
TaskResultSchema expects timestamp as a Date; if the service layer emits ISO strings, Zod parsing will fail. Consider z.preprocess to coerce strings to Date.

tasks.description: Add cascade-blocking detection in task complexity validator
tasks.context: In backend/src/services/task-complexity-validator.ts, implement dependency graph traversal. Detect cycles and long dependency chains (>4 levels). Mark tasks with cascadeRisk=true if they block 3+ other tasks. Return validation error with specific blocking task IDs.

tasks.description: Persist rejection metadata and auto-decomposition flags in orchestrator config
tasks.context: In backend/src/services/orchestrator-config.ts, add fields: rejectionCount, rejectionReasons[], autoDecomposed, cascadeRisk. Add methods saveRejectionState() and getRejectionHistory(). Persist to Redis with 7-day TTL for audit trail.

tasks.description: Wire cascade detection into quality gate enforcement
tasks.context: In backend/src/services/quality-gate-config.ts, add cascade-blocking check to validation rules. Tasks with cascadeRisk=true get priority boost (+10). Tasks blocking >5 others trigger immediate CEO alert via logger. Add bypass flag for emergency overrides.

tasks.description: Add CEO alert logging for auto-decomposition triggers
tasks.context: In backend/src/utils/logger.ts, add logAutoDecomposition() method. Include task ID, rejection count, reasons, and new subtask IDs. Format for Telegram/email alerts. Log to separate auto-decomposition.log file with JSON structure for analysis.
